rulings.Case Title: DONALD PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant ExxonMobil Oil Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the premises owner/contractor liability cause of action and granted as to the civil battery cause of action. As to the premises owner/contractor liability cause of action, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to plaintiff. As to the civil battery cause of action, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable under a civil battery claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD POBLITZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Allan Automatic Sprinkler Corporation¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is overruled. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs¿ complaint fails for uncertainty or fails to state a cause of action against defendant. The Court will not take judicial notice of the Articles of Incorporation attached by defendant. The Court will not take judicial notice of the website printout attached by plaintiffs. Plaintiffs¿ request to the Court for sanctions and/or discipline is denied. Defendant has 10 days to answer the complaint. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
